prp.objective: Create internal/provider/builtin.go defining `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returning the SIX compiled-in provider manifests from external_deps.md §B.1–B.6 with the four §C corrections applied — pi (§B.1, byte-identical to commit-pi), claude (§B.2, fuller 5-flag set incl. --disable-slash-commands + --no-chrome), gemini (§B.3, positional delivery), opencode (§B.4), codex (§B.5 CORRECTED: prompt_delivery="stdin" + --ephemeral), cursor (§B.6 command="agent", --mode ask --trust). Each field set exactly per §B.1–B.6, values BYTE-IDENTICAL to the already-merged sixBuiltinManifests() oracle in manifest_test.go. Create builtin_test.go that (a) asserts each manifest TOML round-trips via go-toml/v2 (normalize nil<->empty slice first — verified gotcha), (b) spot-checks the §C-corrected fields (claude BareFlags is the 7-element slice encoding 5 flags — NOT len==5; codex PromptDelivery=="stdin" + contains --ephemeral; gemini PrintFlag==""). Refactor sixBuiltinManifests() in manifest_test.go to `return Builtins()` (single source of truth). Run `go mod tidy` to flip go-toml/v2 to a direct dependency (now safe — it has an importer). DOCS impact: Mode A — a godoc comment ON Builtins() lists the six built-ins, notes the §C corrections, points to §12.8 for user-defined providers (manifest.go still owns the package doc — do NOT duplicate it).
prp.context:
# PRP — P1.M2.T3.S1: internal/provider/builtin.go — six verified manifests

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Ship the six compiled-in provider manifests as data — a single exported `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returning pi, claude, gemini, opencode, codex, cursor, each field set exactly per external_deps.md §B.1–B.6 (live `--help` verified 2026-06-30) with the four §C corrections applied. This is the production source-of-truth the registry (M2.T3.S2) layers user overrides onto, the config-override base (M5.T3.S1), and the reference manifests (M8.T1.S1). The values are CONFIRMED, not stubbed (all six agents installed on the host).

**Deliverable**: Two new files + two small modifications in the existing `internal/provider` package:
- `internal/provider/builtin.go` — `package provider` (plain line; manifest.go owns the package doc) + `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returning the six manifests, with a comprehensive function godoc (Mode A docs).
- `internal/provider/builtin_test.go` — `package provider`, stdlib `testing` + `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`; TOML round-trip test + §C-corrected-field spot-checks.
- MODIFY `internal/provider/manifest_test.go`: change the body of the existing `sixBuiltinManifests()` helper to `return Builtins()` (DRY; the golden Render table now exercises the REAL production manifests). Its doc comment already says builtin.go (M2.T3.S1) will own them.
- MODIFY `go.mod` via `go mod tidy`: go-toml/v2 flips from `// indirect` to a direct `require` (it now has an importer: builtin_test.go).

**Success Definition**: `go test ./internal/provider/` and `go test ./...` pass; `Builtins()` returns a 6-key map whose values are byte-identical to the existing `sixBuiltinManifests()` oracle; each manifest TOML-marshals and unmarshals losslessly (after nil/empty normalization); `go build`, `go vet`, `gofmt -l` all clean; `go mod tidy` leaves go.mod with go-toml as direct + cobra direct + mousetrap/pflag indirect.

## Why

- **The provider system's data keystone.** The Manifest type + Render algorithm (M2.T1.S1) and the output parser (M2.T2.S1) are done; this task supplies the actual provider DATA they operate on. The registry (M2.T3.S2) merges user config onto `Builtins()` field-by-field, config (M5.T3.S1) uses these as the override base, and the executor (M2.T4.S1) renders+runs them. No downstream task can integrate without these six manifests.
- **Values are verified, not guessed.** external_deps.md §B cross-checked every flag against live `--help` on 2026-06-30, and §C records the FOUR corrections that improve fidelity over the PRD's illustrative TOML (codex stdin+--ephemeral; claude's fuller 5-flag set; gemini positional carried-forward; cursor unchanged). Hard-coding these now means the v1 binary works with zero config for six real agents.
- **TOML round-trip = tag-correctness proof.** Asserting each manifest survives a go-toml/v2 marshal→unmarshal validates the `toml:"..."` struct tags on `Manifest` — which the config loader (M5) depends on. This is the earliest, cheapest place to prove the schema is serializable.
- **Single source of truth.** An identical copy of these six manifests already lives in `manifest_test.go:sixBuiltinManifests()` (the Render golden oracle). Promoting it to `Builtins()` and delegating the test helper removes a drift risk: the Render tests then exercise the production manifests, not a hand-maintained duplicate.

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/provider/builtin.go`, plain `package provider` line (manifest.go line 1 owns the `// Package provider` doc — do NOT repeat it; duplicate-package-comment is a lint/vet issue). Import only `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2` is NOT needed here (builtin.go holds Go literals, no TOML I/O); builtin.go imports NOTHING beyond the package itself (the manifests are `Manifest{...}` struct literals). Actually builtin.go needs no imports at all — it only constructs `map[string]Manifest`.
2. `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returning a fresh map literal with keys "pi","claude","gemini","opencode","codex","cursor" and values EXACTLY matching the existing `manifest_test.go:sixBuiltinManifests()` oracle (copy those struct literals verbatim). Authoritative field values (see Implementation Blueprint for the full literals):
   - **pi**: Name/Detect/Command="pi"; PromptDelivery=DeliveryStdin; PrintFlag="-p"; ModelFlag="--model"; DefaultModel="glm-5-turbo"; SystemPromptFlag="--system-prompt"; ProviderFlag="--provider"; DefaultProvider=""; BareFlags=["--no-tools","--no-extensions","--no-skills","--no-prompt-templates","--no-context-files","--no-session"]; Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true; RetryInstruction="Output ONLY the commit message. No preamble, no markdown, no quotes." (the §12.1 default).
   - **claude**: Name/Detect/Command="claude"; PromptDelivery=DeliveryStdin; PrintFlag="-p"; ModelFlag="--model"; DefaultModel="sonnet"; SystemPromptFlag="--system-prompt"; ProviderFlag=""; BareFlags=["--setting-sources","","--tools","","--disable-slash-commands","--no-chrome","--no-session-persistence"] (7 SLICE elements = 5 logical flags); Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true.
   - **gemini**: Name/Detect/Command="gemini"; PromptDelivery=DeliveryPositional; PrintFlag=""; ModelFlag="-m"; DefaultModel="gemini-2.5-pro"; SystemPromptFlag=""; ProviderFlag=""; BareFlags=["--approval-mode","default"]; Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true.
   - **opencode**: Name/Detect/Command="opencode"; Subcommand=["run"]; PromptDelivery=DeliveryPositional; PrintFlag=""; ModelFlag="-m"; DefaultModel=""; SystemPromptFlag=""; ProviderFlag=""; BareFlags=nil; Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true.
   - **codex** (§C.2 CORRECTED): Name/Detect/Command="codex"; Subcommand=["exec"]; PromptDelivery=DeliveryStdin (NOT positional); PrintFlag=""; ModelFlag="-m"; DefaultModel=""; SystemPromptFlag=""; ProviderFlag=""; BareFlags=["--sandbox","read-only","--ask-for-approval","never","--ephemeral"]; Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true.
   - **cursor**: Name="cursor"; Detect="agent"; Command="agent"; PromptDelivery=DeliveryPositional; PrintFlag="-p"; ModelFlag="--model"; DefaultModel=""; SystemPromptFlag=""; ProviderFlag=""; BareFlags=["--mode","ask","--trust"]; Output=OutputRaw; StripCodeFence=true.
   Return a NEW map each call (callers — registry M2.T3.S2 — must not be able to mutate a shared backing map).
3. Doc comment on `Builtins()` (Mode A): list the six built-in provider names; state these are compiled-in defaults verified against live `--help` (external_deps.md §B, 2026-06-30) with the four §C corrections; note this map is the base for the registry override-merge (§12.8 / M2.T3.S2), the config-override base (M5.T3.S1), and the reference manifests (M8.T1.S1); point to PRD §12.8 for adding user-defined providers. This godoc feeds PROVIDERS.md (M8.T4.S2). Do NOT create README/docs/providers TOML.
4. NEW file `internal/provider/builtin_test.go`, `package provider`, imports `testing`, `reflect`, and `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`. Tests:
   - `TestBuiltins_KeysAndCount`: `len(Builtins()) == 6`; the key set is exactly {pi,claude,gemini,opencode,codex,cursor}; calling twice returns equal but independently-addressable maps (no shared mutation).
   - `TestBuiltins_MatchesOracle`: `reflect.DeepEqual(Builtins(), sixBuiltinManifests())` is TRUE (proves Builtins() == the existing Render golden oracle). NOTE: after the Task-2 refactor `sixBuiltinManifests()` just returns `Builtins()`, so this test instead asserts Builtins() against a LOCAL copy of the expected map (or, equivalently, keep a separate expected-literal in the test) to retain an independent check — implement as a local `want := map[string]Manifest{...}` mirroring §B and assert equality. (The refactor means sixBuiltinManifests() no longer holds an independent copy; the test must carry its own expected literal for an independent oracle.)
   - `TestBuiltins_TOMLRoundTrip`: for each entry, `toml.Marshal(m)` succeeds; `toml.Unmarshal` into a fresh `Manifest`; assert equality after NORMALIZING nil<->empty slices (see gotcha §4 — go-toml/v2 unmarshals an absent/empty array as `[]string{}`, so `reflect.DeepEqual` on the raw structs fails for opencode/pi). Provide a `normalizeEmpty` helper (`if len(s)==0 { return nil }` applied to Subcommand+BareFlags on BOTH sides) then `reflect.DeepEqual`. (Alternative/complementary: assert `bytes.Equal(toml.Marshal(m), toml.Marshal(back))` — idempotency — which is immune to nil/empty; verified PASS.)
   - `TestBuiltins_SpotChecks` (the §C corrections): `len(claude.BareFlags) == 7` AND it contains "--disable-slash-commands" and "--no-chrome" (the §C.1 additions); codex `PromptDelivery == "stdin"` (§C.2, NOT positional) AND `codex.BareFlags` contains "--ephemeral" (§C.2 added); gemini `PrintFlag == ""`; cursor `Command == "agent"` AND `Detect == "agent"`.
5. MODIFY `internal/provider/manifest_test.go`: replace the body of `sixBuiltinManifests()` with `return Builtins()` (one line; keep the function signature and its doc comment, lightly updated to say it now delegates to the production Builtins()). The Render golden table (TestRender_GoldenProviders etc.) is UNCHANGED and still passes because Builtins() returns byte-identical manifests. This is the ONLY change to manifest_test.go.
6. Run `go mod tidy` once (single command) so go.mod reflects go-toml/v2 as a direct dependency (builtin_test.go imports it). Verified safe: tidy moves go-toml into the direct `require` block, keeps cobra direct, keeps mousetrap+pflag indirect, go.sum unchanged.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/provider/builtin.go` exists with a plain `package provider` line (NO duplicate package doc) and a no-import `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returning a fresh 6-key map whose values are byte-identical to the §B.1–B.6 manifests with the four §C corrections (codex stdin+--ephemeral; claude 7-element/5-flag bare_flags; gemini positional/PrintFlag=""; cursor command="agent").
- [ ] `Builtins()` carries a Mode-A godoc listing the six providers + §12.8 pointer (feeds PROVIDERS.md M8.T4.S2); no README/docs/providers TOML created.
- [ ] `internal/provider/builtin_test.go` asserts: 6 keys; TOML round-trip (nil/empty normalized) for all six; spot-checks claude len==7 + the two added flags, codex stdin + --ephemeral, gemini PrintFlag=="", cursor command/detect=="agent".
- [ ] `manifest_test.go:sixBuiltinManifests()` delegates to `Builtins()`; the existing Render golden table still passes unchanged.
- [ ] `go mod tidy` run; go.mod shows go-toml/v2 as a direct `require`.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/provider/` and `go test ./...` exit 0; `go build ./internal/provider/`, `go vet ./internal/provider/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/provider/` empty.

## All Needed Context

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
package provider  
func Builtins() map[string]Manifest {
    return map[string]Manifest{
        "pi":       { ...§B.1... },
        "claude":   { ...§B.2 + §C.1... },
        "gemini":   { ...§B.3... },
        "opencode": { ...§B.4... },
        "codex":    { ...§B.5 + §C.2 (stdin, --ephemeral)... },
        "cursor":   { ...§B.6... },
    }
}

```

### Implementation Tasks (ordered by dependencies)
```yaml
Task 1: CREATE internal/provider/builtin.go
  - Plain `package provider` line (manifest.go owns the package doc — do NOT repeat it).
  - NO imports (only map[string]Manifest struct literals referencing the package's own types/consts).
  - IMPLEMENT func Builtins() map[string]Manifest returning a FRESH 6-key map; copy the field values verbatim from the existing manifest_test.go:sixBuiltinManifests() oracle (== external_deps §B + §C corrections). Use the DeliveryStdin/DeliveryPositional/OutputRaw constants (not raw strings) for readability/consistency with manifest.go.
  - The literal order per provider is spelled out in "What §2"; codex uses DeliveryStdin + includes "--ephemeral"; claude uses the 7-element BareFlags; gemini PrintFlag=""; cursor Detect/Command="agent".
  - Doc comment (Mode A) on Builtins(): list the six names, note live-help verification + the four §C corrections, state it is the base for registry overrides (M2.T3.S2) + config-override base (M5.T3.S1) + reference manifests (M8.T1.S1), and point to PRD §12.8 for user-defined providers.
Task 2: MODIFY internal/provider/manifest_test.go
  - Replace the BODY of sixBuiltinManifests() with `return Builtins()`. Keep the signature + lightly update its doc comment to note it now delegates to the production source. The Render golden table (TestRender_GoldenProviders et al.) stays byte-for-byte unchanged and still passes.
Task 3: CREATE internal/provider/builtin_test.go
  - `package provider`; imports: testing, reflect, bytes (if using idempotency assert), github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2.
  - TestBuiltins_KeysAndCount: len==6; keys == {pi,claude,gemini,opencode,codex,cursor}; two calls are DeepEqual and independently addressable.
  - TestBuiltins_MatchesOracle: a LOCAL `want := map[string]Manifest{...}` mirroring §B (independent of sixBuiltinManifests(), which now delegates); assert reflect.DeepEqual(Builtins(), want).
  - TestBuiltins_TOMLRoundTrip: for each entry, toml.Marshal succeeds; toml.Unmarshal into a fresh Manifest; normalizeEmpty (nil when len==0) on Subcommand+BareFlags of BOTH; reflect.DeepEqual == true. (Optionally also assert Marshal(orig) bytes == Marshal(roundtripped) bytes.)
  - TestBuiltins_SpotChecks: len(claude.BareFlags)==7 and contains --disable-slash-commands + --no-chrome; codex PromptDelivery=="stdin" and BareFlags contains --ephemeral; gemini PrintFlag==""; cursor Command=="agent" and Detect=="agent".
  - NAMING: TestBuiltins_<Scenario>; use t.Run subtests where helpful.
Task 4: RUN `go mod tidy` (single command) so go.mod lists go-toml/v2 as a direct require. Verify it did NOT remove cobra/pflag/mousetrap.
Task 5: VERIFY via the validation gates IN ORDER (build→vet→fmt→tidy→test). Fix any failure before declaring done. Confirm scope: only builtin.go + builtin_test.go created; manifest_test.go body-changed only in sixBuiltinManifests(); go.mod changed only by tidy; manifest.go/parse*.go/main.go/Makefile/go.sum untouched; no README/docs/providers TOML; the `// Package provider` doc appears exactly once (in manifest.go).

```

### Implementation Patterns
```go
func normalizeEmpty(s []string) []string {
    if len(s) == 0 {
        return nil
    }
    return s
}
func manifestsEqual(a, b Manifest) bool {
    a.Subcommand = normalizeEmpty(a.Subcommand); b.Subcommand = normalizeEmpty(b.Subcommand)
    a.BareFlags = normalizeEmpty(a.BareFlags); b.BareFlags = normalizeEmpty(b.BareFlags)
    return reflect.DeepEqual(a, b)
}

```

### Integration Points
```yaml
PACKAGE: github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/provider
PRODUCES: provider.Builtins() map[string]Manifest
CONSUMERS (downstream, DO NOT implement here):
  - internal/provider/registry.go (M2.T3.S2): base = Builtins(); merge user overrides field-by-field.
  - config override base (M5.T3.S1): user [provider.<name>] overrides these defaults.
  - reference manifests (M8.T1.S1): emit these as on-disk TOML references.
  - manifest_test.go (THIS task, modified): sixBuiltinManifests() -> return Builtins().
NOT HERE: agent execution (M2.T4.S1 executor), config TOML loading (M5), PATH detection (M2.T3.S2 registry).
DOCS: Mode A — godoc on Builtins() (lists six providers + §12.8 pointer; feeds PROVIDERS.md M8.T4.S2). manifest.go keeps the package doc.

```

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** A godoc comment on the `Builtins()` function lists the six built-in providers, notes they are verified against live `--help` (external_deps.md §B, with the four §C corrections), states this is the base for registry/config overrides and reference manifests, and points to PRD §12.8 for adding user-defined providers. This is the only documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml (the PROVIDERS.md surface is synced in M8.T4.S2 from this godoc). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped; note manifest.go (not builtin.go) owns the package doc to avoid a duplicate-package-comment violation.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT assert `len(claude.BareFlags) == 5` — the slice has 7 elements (5 logical flags); assert ==7 (or count 5 leading-dash tokens).
- ❌ Do NOT compare TOML round-trip with raw reflect.DeepEqual — go-toml/v2 turns nil slices into []string{}; normalize nil<->empty first (or compare re-marshaled bytes).
- ❌ Do NOT repeat the `// Package provider` doc in builtin.go — manifest.go owns it; use a Builtins() godoc for Mode A instead.
- ❌ Do NOT set codex PromptDelivery="positional" — §C.2 corrects it to "stdin" and adds --ephemeral.
- ❌ Do NOT make Builtins() return a package-level shared map — return a fresh map each call (registry mutates a merge copy).
- ❌ Do NOT import go-toml in builtin.go (only builtin_test.go needs it); do NOT run any agent in tests.
- ❌ Do NOT skip `go mod tidy` — go-toml/v2 is now a direct dependency and go.mod should reflect it (tidy is verified safe).
- ❌ Do NOT touch manifest.go, parse.go, parse_test.go, main.go, Makefile, or go.sum; do NOT redefine Manifest/Render; do NOT create README/docs/providers TOML.

Create `internal/provider/builtin_test.go` as `package provider` importing `testing`, `reflect`, `bytes` (optional), and `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`. Tests: (1) `TestBuiltins_KeysAndCount` — `len(Builtins())==6`; key set is exactly {pi,claude,gemini,opencode,codex,cursor}; two calls are `reflect.DeepEqual` and independently addressable. (2) `TestBuiltins_MatchesOracle` — declare a LOCAL `want := map[string]Manifest{...}` that mirrors external_deps.md §B independently of `sixBuiltinManifests()` (which now delegates to `Builtins()`, so it is no longer an independent oracle); assert `reflect.DeepEqual(Builtins(), want)`. (3) `TestBuiltins_TOMLRoundTrip` — for each entry: `toml.Marshal(m)` succeeds; `toml.Unmarshal(buf,&back)` into a fresh Manifest succeeds; assert equality AFTER normalizing nil<->empty slices (provide `normalizeEmpty` returning nil when len==0, applied to Subcommand+BareFlags on BOTH sides, then `reflect.DeepEqual`) — this is required because go-toml/v2 unmarshals an absent/empty array as a NON-nil `[]string{}`, which would otherwise fail for opencode (BareFlags=nil) and pi (Subcommand=nil); optionally also assert `bytes.Equal(toml.Marshal(m), toml.Marshal(back))` (idempotency). (4) `TestBuiltins_SpotChecks` (the §C corrections) — `len(claude.BareFlags)==7` AND it contains "--disable-slash-commands" and "--no-chrome"; `codex.PromptDelivery=="stdin"` AND `codex.BareFlags` contains "--ephemeral"; `gemini.PrintFlag==""`; `cursor.Command=="agent"` AND `cursor.Detect=="agent"`. Use `t.Run` subtests where helpful; `Test*` naming.

Run `go mod tidy` (single command) so `go.mod` lists `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2` as a DIRECT `require` (builtin_test.go imports it, making it no longer indirect). Verify tidy did NOT remove cobra (direct) or mousetrap/pflag (indirect) and left go.sum unchanged.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/provider/` (compiles), `go vet ./internal/provider/` (clean, incl. no duplicate-package-comment), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/provider/)"` (no unformatted files), `go mod tidy` (already run; idempotent), `go test ./internal/provider/` (PASS — keys/count, round-trip, spot-checks, AND the existing Render golden table via the delegated sixBuiltinManifests()), `go test ./...` (whole-module green; internal/ui + provider pass). Then confirm scope boundaries: only `builtin.go` + `builtin_test.go` created; `manifest_test.go` changed only inside `sixBuiltinManifests()`; `go.mod` changed only by tidy; `manifest.go`, `parse.go`, `parse_test.go`, `main.go`, `Makefile`, `go.sum` untouched; no README/docs/providers TOML created; the `// Package provider` doc appears exactly once (in manifest.go).

successCriteria.description: `internal/provider/builtin.go` exists with a PLAIN `package provider` line (NO duplicate package doc — manifest.go owns it) and needs no imports; `func Builtins() map[string]Manifest` returns a fresh 6-key map.

`Builtins()` returns manifests BYTE-IDENTICAL to the existing `manifest_test.go:sixBuiltinManifests()` oracle and to external_deps.md §B.1–§B.6 with the four §C corrections applied: pi (stdin, 6 no-* bare_flags, §12.1 retry_instruction), claude (7-element BareFlags = 5 logical flags incl. --disable-slash-commands + --no-chrome), gemini (positional, PrintFlag=""), opencode (Subcommand=["run"], nil BareFlags), codex (§C.2: PromptDelivery=DeliveryStdin NOT positional, + --ephemeral), cursor (Detect/Command="agent", --mode ask --trust).

`Builtins()` carries a Mode-A godoc listing the six providers, noting live-help verification + the four §C corrections, stating it is the base for registry/config overrides + reference manifests, and pointing to PRD §12.8 for user-defined providers; no README/docs/providers TOML created.
